## Summary
<!-- One or two sentences: what does this PR do? -->

## Linked Issues
<!-- Use `Refs #...` for tracking/parent/umbrella issues. Use `Closes #...` only if merging this PR should close that issue. If no issue exists, explain why. -->

## What This Actually Changes
<!-- Before/after framing: what was true before this PR, what's different after, and why that gap mattered.
Concrete example > abstract description where possible (see distcc-ng#5 for the style this is modeled on). -->

## What This PR Fixes / Adds
<!-- The specific bug, gap, or feature this addresses. If this is a partial/scoped fix, say so explicitly and
name what's intentionally deferred (with a Refs link to the follow-up issue if one exists). -->

## What Changed In Code
<!-- File-by-file or area-by-area: what actually changed, briefly. Not a restatement of the diff --
call out anything non-obvious a reviewer should know before reading the diff itself. -->

## Why This Matters For Users / Operators
<!-- lancache-ng is network infrastructure software (DNS, DHCP, TLS interception, Docker startup, cache
correctness, local network availability). Explain the operator-visible consequence of the bug/gap this
closes, or of NOT merging this PR -- not just the technical mechanism. -->

## Scope Boundaries
<!-- What this PR deliberately does NOT touch, even if related. Keeps review focused and prevents
scope creep from being silently absorbed into an unrelated change. -->

## Risk / Rollback / Follow-up
<!-- State the remaining risk, how to roll back if needed, and any follow-up work that should stay visible in the PR body. -->

## Local Scope Evidence
<!-- List the files this PR actually touches (e.g. from `git diff --stat`). If a related file was
deliberately NOT touched, say so and why. -->


locally, say so and explain why (see AGENTS.md's Required Validation section for what's expected
per change type: shell, Rust, Dockerfile/Compose, workflow, setup/migration, DNS, proxy/cache). -->

## Changelog
<!-- User-facing summary for release notes: user-visible behavior, operational impact,
validation performed, and any explicit follow-up issue. Keep this section updated as the
PR evolves -- edit this body directly rather than adding new comments for progress/changelog
updates. Do NOT edit CHANGELOG.md in this PR: every simultaneously-open PR editing the same
"### Fixed"-style heading guarantees a merge conflict with every other one. CHANGELOG.md is
written automatically at release time: release-drafter.yml drafts a GitHub Release from each
merged PR's title/label, and update-changelog.yaml writes that release's notes into
CHANGELOG.md once it is published (see CONTRIBUTING.md's "Releasing Changes to CHANGELOG.md").
Give this PR a clear title and the right labels so the auto-drafted release notes read well --
this section is for the PR body/review, not a manual CHANGELOG.md edit. -->
